message:
refactor(error): introduce Upgrade variant and retire raw Other(anyhow) wrappers

Several call sites were converting arbitrary errors into
`BeadsError::Other(anyhow::anyhow!(...))`, which flattened into the generic
"Other" code and lost the operation-specific context that the structured
error layer uses to drive exit codes, JSON error bodies, and diagnostics.

- Add `BeadsError::Upgrade { reason }` and a `BeadsError::upgrade()`
  constructor. Structured rendering maps the variant to `IoError`
  (exit code 8) with `context.operation = "upgrade"`, matching how
  network/self-update failures have historically been reported by the
  self_update crate paths.
- Route both self-update failure branches in `upgrade::execute_upgrade`
  and `map_update_error` through the new variant so the long-form archive
  hint is preserved while the error classification stays intentional.
- Replace ad-hoc anyhow wrapping in:
    * `OptionExt::ok_or_context` — now yields `BeadsError::internal` so
      missing-context failures surface as InternalError instead of Other.
    * `SqliteStorage::get_issue_from_conn` — consistency mismatches are
      genuine internal invariants, not opaque "Other" errors.
    * `sync::restore_foreign_keys_after_import` — "FK enforcement still
      off" is internal; orphaned-row detection is reclassified via
      `BeadsError::validation("jsonl import", ...)` so the CLI exit code
      and JSON code reflect import validation rather than an unlabelled bag.
- Add a regression test asserting the Upgrade variant produces exit code 8
  and surfaces `operation=upgrade` in the structured payload.

No functional behaviour change beyond error classification; existing
call sites that matched on `BeadsError::Other` did not rely on these paths.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
